name: Route through the center and coast of Gijón — circular from the station
name_original: Ruta por el centro y costa de Gijón — circular desde la estación
region_intro: Jogging routes Gijón Xixón offer a diverse landscape for runners, blending urban green spaces with extensive coastal paths and natural inland scenery. The region is characterized by its long coastline, featuring beaches and cliffs, alongside several river paths and significant urban parks. Runners can experience both sea views and tranquil, natural environments close to the city center. Best jogging routes around Gijón/Xixón The most popular jogging route is Parque del Cerro to the Monumento a la Madre del Emigrante via the coastal trail of Gijón, a 4.6 miles (7.3 km) trail that takes 46 minutes to complete. This route follows the scenic coastal path, offering views of the Bay of Gijón. Another top favourite among local runners is Route through the Jardín Botánico Atlántico to Deva from the Edificio Polivalente, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.3 km) path. This route traverses the botanical garden and extends towards the natural area of Deva. Local runners also love the Route through the center and coast of Gijón — circular from the station, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through the city center and along the coast, often completed in about 31 minutes. Jogging around Gijón/Xixón is defined by its coastal paths, river paths, and urban parks, offering a variety of terrains. The network provides options for different ability levels, from flat promenades to more undulating trails. The routes in Gijón/Xixón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 48 reviews. More than 4000 runners have used komoot to explore Gijón/Xixón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Gijón/Xixón offers a wide selection of running routes, with nearly 150 trails available for you to explore. These routes cater to various preferences, from easy strolls to more challenging runs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Gijón/Xixón?
A: You'll find a diverse range of terrain in Gijón/Xixón. The region is known for its extensive coastal paths, offering flat promenades and scenic clifftop trails. Additionally, there are tranquil river paths and lush urban parks, providing a mix of paved and natural surfaces. For those seeking more challenge, areas like Monte Deva offer undulating trails through forests and meadows.

Q: Are there any scenic coastal running paths in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Absolutely! Gijón/Xixón is renowned for its spectacular coastal running paths. The &lt;strong&gt;Cervigón Path&lt;/strong&gt; is a prominent route stretching along cliffs and beaches, offering breathtaking sea views. You can also enjoy a lively run along the 3-kilometer promenade of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/337074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Lorenzo beach&lt;/a&gt;, which transitions from urban landscapes to single-family homes. Another option is the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056047692/playa-de-serin-islote-de-tortuga-senda-del-cerbigon-y-parque-del-cabo-san-lorenzo-por-la-costa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Serín, Islote de Tortuga, Senda del Cervigón y Parque del Cabo San Lorenzo por la costa&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 6.1 km of coastal scenery.

Q: Can I find running routes along rivers or through green spaces in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Yes, Gijón/Xixón features several beautiful river paths and greenways. The &lt;strong&gt;Piles River Path&lt;/strong&gt; is a significant green corridor extending for 19 kilometers, perfect for a tranquil run alongside the river. Other options include the scenic &lt;strong&gt;Ñora River Path&lt;/strong&gt; with its charming wooden bridges, and the &lt;strong&gt;Peñafrancia Path&lt;/strong&gt;, which follows the river near the university campus.

Q: Are there any parks in Gijón/Xixón suitable for jogging?
A: Gijón/Xixón boasts several excellent urban parks for jogging. &lt;strong&gt;Parque de Isabel La Católica&lt;/strong&gt;, near San Lorenzo beach, offers wide, tree-lined walkways. Other notable parks include &lt;strong&gt;Parque de los Pericones&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Begoña Park&lt;/strong&gt; in the city center, both providing ample space for exercise. &lt;strong&gt;Rinconín Park&lt;/strong&gt; is also a great recreation area along the coastal path.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Gijón/Xixón?
A: The jogging routes in Gijón/Xixón are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 48 reviews. Nearly 5000 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from coastal views to tranquil river paths, and the well-maintained trails suitable for all levels.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed run?
A: Yes, Gijón/Xixón offers 13 easy jogging routes perfect for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed pace. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056054608/route-through-the-center-and-coast-of-gijon-circular-from-the-station" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route through the center and coast of Gijón — circular from the station&lt;/a&gt;, a 5 km trail that takes about 31 minutes to complete, offering a pleasant mix of urban and coastal scenery.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners looking for a challenge, Gijón/Xixón has 47 difficult routes. The &lt;strong&gt;Monte Deva Recreational Area&lt;/strong&gt; provides a network of paths through forests and peaks with varying difficulties and panoramic views. A longer, more demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24687947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Gijón – Cider Tree loop from Viesques&lt;/a&gt;, a 15.2 km route with moderate elevation changes.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along the running routes?
A: Many running routes in Gijón/Xixón pass by significant landmarks and viewpoints. Along the coast, you can see Eduardo Chillida's iconic sculpture &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4885149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elogio del Horizonte&lt;/a&gt; and enjoy views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5575818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Cervigón&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/473306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Providencia Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. In the city, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5042924" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Marqués de San Esteban&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Yes, many of Gijón/Xixón's green spaces and flatter paths are suitable for families. The &lt;strong&gt;Piles River Path&lt;/strong&gt;, with its playgrounds and picnic areas, is an excellent choice. Urban parks like &lt;strong&gt;Parque de Isabel La Católica&lt;/strong&gt; also offer wide, safe paths for a family jog or stroll.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Many of the natural and green space routes in Gijón/Xixón are dog-friendly, provided your dog is kept on a leash and you clean up after them. The extensive &lt;strong&gt;Piles River Path&lt;/strong&gt; and the trails within &lt;strong&gt;Monte Deva Recreational Area&lt;/strong&gt; are popular choices for running with canine companions, offering plenty of space and natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Gijón/Xixón?
A: Yes, you can find several circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056054608/route-through-the-center-and-coast-of-gijon-circular-from-the-station" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route through the center and coast of Gijón — circular from the station&lt;/a&gt; offers a convenient loop through the city and along the coastline. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24687947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor de Gijón – Cider Tree loop from Viesques&lt;/a&gt; is another circular option for a longer run.

Q: Are there running routes that offer views of the Jardín Botánico Atlántico?
A: Yes, you can incorporate the beautiful Jardín Botánico Atlántico into your run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056063314/route-through-the-jardin-botanico-atlantico-to-deva-from-the-edificio-polivalente" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route through the Jardín Botánico Atlántico to Deva from the Edificio Polivalente&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.3 km path that traverses the botanical garden before extending towards the natural area of Deva, offering a blend of cultivated beauty and natural landscapes.
tips:
The Plaza Mayor of Gijón is a historic square located in the centre of the Asturian city of Gijón, Spain. It is known for housing the Casa Consistorial, which is the main seat of the City Council.
The square is located between San Lorenzo beach and Plazuela del Marqués, in the Cimadevilla neighbourhood. It was designed in 1858 by Andrés Coello and later modified in 1864 by Luis Céspedes and Lucas María Palacios.
One of the most photographed places in the city. A spectacular and striking sculpture made with cider bottles, located in the marina.
One of the three beaches that Gijón has with an artificial beach created years ago. Close to any trade or restaurant.

waypoints.name: Árbol de la Sidra (Cider Bottle Sculpture)
